title: Manisuris altissima
taxonomy: Manisuris altissima is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Manisuris, which is a small group of plants native to China. Manisuris altissima is an evergreen tree that grows in the subtropical and tropical regions. Its classification is further supported by molecular studies, which confirm its position within the Annonaceae family. The exact classification of Manisuris altissima is still being researched and refined by botanists.

constituents: Manisuris altissima contains active constituents such as alkaloids and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. However, specific information on its active constituents is limited and requires further research. The plant's alkaloids have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Iridoid glycosides present in Manisuris altissima may exhibit hepatoprotective and antioxidant effects. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the plant's active constituents and their potential therapeutic applications.
